scope: repositories/antonoly/anymodel: scripts/brand-patch.mjs, scripts/brand-patches.json, test/brand-patch.test.mjs (cli.js = generated string-data)
method: code-review skill, high effort — 3 parallel finder angles (applier correctness, manifest/adaptive-expr safety, test coverage) + verification
summary: 5 confirmed findings (1 high-severity, converged by 3 agents), all FIXED. 2 informational (no defect).

findings.summary: Drift-masking when `to` is a substring of `from` (model-updated-opus/sonnet): an upstream version bump (Opus 4.6 -> 4.7) makes the exact `from` miss while `toPresent` stays true, so the idempotency branch silently skips it and ships unbranded text.
findings.resolution: Added VENDOR_DENYLIST anti-regression sweep (version-tolerant regexes, verified 0 matches in branded bundle) that runs in apply AND check mode and flags residual vendor phrases the exact anchor misses. New test 'vendor denylist catches a version-bumped string the exact anchor misses' proves it.

findings.summary: Partial-apply-on-drift wrote a half-branded bundle: non-check mode persisted patches 1..N-1 even when patch N drifted, making the 'Refusing to corrupt the bundle' message false.
findings.resolution: Apply mode is now ATOMIC: `if (drift.length) return` before any write. Any patch-drift or denylist hit leaves the bundle untouched.

findings.summary: `--check` mode never ran `node --check`, so the verify-only CI gate confirmed branding coverage but not bundle parseability — contradicting the header's parse-safety claim.
findings.resolution: check mode now runs `node --check` on the bundle and reports a parse failure as drift. The live-bundle test exercises this path.

findings.summary: Empty/short manifest would make the whole suite (incl. live-bundle gate) pass vacuously.
findings.resolution: Added 'manifest is non-trivially populated' test asserting BRAND_PATCHES.length >= 40.

findings.summary: Parse-safety guarantee (node --check + throw-on-unparseable) had zero automated coverage; manifest fixtures are invalid JS by design.
findings.resolution: Added `opts.patches` override and a test 'apply mode keeps a valid bundle valid, and throws on an unparseable result' covering both the success and the throw path with valid JS fixtures.

findings.summary: ANYMODEL_MODEL is injected only on the proxy path; on `anymodel claude` direct launch it is undefined, so adaptive strings render the 'anymodel' fallback rather than a model name.
findings.resolution: Correct behavior — the direct path has no selected backend model. The `||"anymodel"` fallback (and the one ternary entry) handle undefined safely; no crash.

findings.summary: The `out.includes(p.to)` assertion is weak for substring-`to` entries (the `to` is already a substring of the pristine `from`).
findings.resolution: The sibling `out.includes(p.from) === false` assertion is the real guard; CR-01's denylist + CR-04's floor add the substantive coverage.

verification.brand_patch_tests: 7/7 pass (incl. denylist, atomic, parse-safety, floor, live-bundle gate)
verification.idempotency: re-run = no-op; --check = clean (61 applied, denylist clean, parses)
